title: Illposedness of incompressible fluids in supercritical Sobolev spaces

thm:1.1.summary: 3D Euler is strongly illposed in $H^s(R^3)$ for 0 < s < 5/2: for any $\varepsilon>0$, there exists smooth divergence-free u₀ with ||u₀$||_{H^s} \le \varepsilon$ such that ||u(t*)||_{Ḣ$^s} \ge \varepsilon$⁻^1 at some $t* \le \varepsilon$.

rem:1.2.summary: Construction is explicit: axisymmetric with large swirl, solution remains smooth at inflation time t*. Norm inflation visible only for s' $\ge$ s.

thm:1.3.summary: 3D Navier-Stokes is strongly illposed in $H^s(R^3)$ for 0 < s < 1/2: same norm inflation construction as Euler but with restricted range.

prop:2.1.summary: If $|\nablau|_{L^\infty} \le$ M on [0,t₀], then $||u(t)||_{W^{k,p}} \le ||u$₀$||_{W^{k,p}} exp(C_{k,p}$ M t). Standard Sobolev propagation under bounded gradient.

lem:2.2.summary: Gronwall's inequality: if $d\eta/dt \le \alpha(t)\eta + \beta(t)$ with nonnegative summable $\alpha, \beta$, then $\eta(t) \leexp(\int\alpha)(\eta(0) + \int\beta)$.

rem:3.1.summary: Initial data u₀ is axisymmetric: vortex ring + rotational flow supported in small torus at distance $~\nu$⁻^1 from origin with cross-section $~\mu$⁻^1.

lem:3.2.summary: The initial data u₀ is smooth, divergence-free, compactly supported, and satisfies ||u₀$||_{W^{k,p}} \le C_{k,p} \varepsilon^2 \mu^{k-s}$ with ||u₀$||_{H^s} \le \varepsilon$ for small $\varepsilon$.

rem:3.3.summary: Only swirl component ū$_\theta$ evolves in time; vortex ring part is stationary. $L^p$ norms almost constant but norms with derivatives grow — this drives Ḣ^s inflation.

prop:3.4.summary:
The approximate solution ū is smooth, divergence-free, axisymmetric, satisfies $W^{k,p}$ bounds, and solves Euler up to a controlled error field Ē with $||
abla^k$ Ē$||_{L^2} \le$ C $\mu^{k-s}(\mu$⁻$^1
u)(\mu^{2-s}
u^{1/2})$.

lem:3.5.summary: Lower bound: ||ū(t*)||_{Ḣ$^s} \ge \varepsilon$⁻^2 at critical time t*. Proves the norm inflation actually occurs for the approximate solution.

prop:4.1.summary: The true Euler solution u exists smoothly up to t* and satisfies ||u - ū$||_{L^\infty([0,t*]$;$H^k)} \le C_{k,\varepsilon} \mu^{k-s}$. Perturbation stays controlled.

lem:4.2.summary: $L^2$ estimate: under bootstrap assumption, $||w(t)||_{L^2} \le C_\varepsilon \mu^{-s}(\mu$⁻$^1\nu)$ where w = u - ū.

lem:4.3.summary: Higher Sobolev estimate by induction: $||\nabla^k w(t)||_{L^2} \le C_{\varepsilon,k} \mu^{k-s}(\mu$⁻$^1\nu)^{\delta_k}$ with $\delta_k = (9 + 10^{-k})/10 > 0$.

edges.note: Main theorem reduces to showing smooth existence up to t*
Needs lower bound on ū(t*) which transfers to u
Same construction with restricted s range
Uses higher Sobolev control for bootstrap improvement
Uses error field bounds from approximate solution
Uses initial data structure and bounds

construction.description: Build axisymmetric initial data as vortex ring + swirl in small torus

approximate_solution.description: Construct ū solving Euler up to controlled error, with explicit $W^{k,p}$ bounds and norm inflation at t*

perturbation_control.description: Bootstrap argument: w = u - ū stays small in all Sobolev norms up to t*

tools.description: Standard PDE tools: Gronwall, Sobolev propagation
